design_tasks.title: Suggestion-card visual spec/audit (chip variant: copper accent, road icon, surface.glass scrim @ 72%, ≥44pt, hidden when route shown)
design_tasks.outcome: A written spec-diff exists at `.spec/design/sprint-01/suggestion-card-spec.md` naming exact token paths for every gap vs the current `SuggestionChips` implementation in `chat-input.tsx`, verifiable by `pnpm tokens:validate` and on-device inspection at the sprint gate.
critical_constraints: NEVER name a hardcoded hex color in the spec — every color reference MUST be a token path (e.g. `semantic.color.accent.default`, `semantic.color.surface.glass`). Any gap where the current code uses a raw hex is the gap the spec must name and flag.

NEVER recommend inline `backgroundColor: 'rgba(..., 0.72)'` for the scrim — the spec MUST reference `surface.glass` (already defined in `tokens/semantic/colors.tokens.json` as `rgba(253,251,248,0.72)` / `rgba(45,34,24,0.72)`) and note the existing `CC` hex-alpha pattern in `route-attachment-card.tsx` as a known legacy anti-pattern NOT to replicate.
NEVER specify a touch target below 44pt — `semantic.control.minTouchTarget` (44) is the floor; the spec must cite this token.
Spec is a WRITE to `.spec/design/sprint-01/suggestion-card-spec.md` ONLY — do NOT modify `chat-input.tsx` or any app source file.
Score rendering is out of scope for this task — that is DESIGN-S01-002.

specification.deliverable: `.spec/design/sprint-01/suggestion-card-spec.md` — a structured diff document with four sections: (1) Current state audit of `SuggestionChips` in `components/chat/chat-input.tsx`; (2) Token gap table listing every property that currently uses a hardcoded value with its required token replacement; (3) Exact prop/style spec for the curated-pill chip variant; (4) Visibility rule spec (gating condition).

chip_variant_spec.background: semantic.color.surface.glass (light: rgba(253,251,248,0.72); dark: rgba(45,34,24,0.72)) — NOT raw hex+opacity
chip_variant_spec.border: borderWidth: semantic.borderWidth.thin (1pt); borderColor: semantic.color.border.glass (light: rgba(255,255,255,0.55); dark: rgba(242,238,232,0.22))
chip_variant_spec.min_height: semantic.control.minTouchTarget (44pt) — current code correctly sets minHeight: 44 but sources it from a magic number; spec requires referencing the token
chip_variant_spec.icon: react-native-paper `Icon` source='road-variant' size=16 color=semantic.color.accent.default (#EE7C2B) — NOT the fallback `?? '#EE7C2B'` pattern; spec must require the token path directly
chip_variant_spec.label_text: semantic.type.body.sm (fontSize 11, lineHeight 16, fontWeight 400); color: semantic.color.onSurface.default; fontWeight override to '500' is acceptable for readability

current_gaps_to_audit: Line 110-116 in chat-input.tsx: `borderColor: semantic.color.border.default` — should be `semantic.color.border.glass` for a glassmorphic chip on the map overlay
Line 110-116: `backgroundColor: semantic.color.surface.default` — should be `semantic.color.surface.glass` (the 72% alpha token, not the opaque surface.default)

Line 127-130: `semantic.color.accent?.default ?? '#EE7C2B'` — the `??` fallback is a gap; accent.default is defined in both light and dark in semantic.tokens.json; the spec must note the guard is unnecessary and the token path is stable
No elevation applied to the curated chip — should use semantic.elevation[2]
The `SuggestionChips` scroll container has no `surface.glass` scrim behind it as a group — spec must note whether a wrapping scrim View is needed or whether individual chip backgrounds suffice
specification.visibility_rule_spec: Chips render only when: (a) `isIdle === true` (phase === 'IDLE'), AND (b) `hasActiveRoute === false` (no `agentActiveOption`), AND (c) `suggestions.length > 0`, AND (d) `!isPlanning`, AND (e) `!chatMode`. The current conditional at line 268 of chat-input.tsx matches this rule. The spec must confirm the condition is correct and note that `hasActiveRoute` is derived from `!!agentActiveOption` in `index.tsx` (line 257).
specification.spec_section_for_building_agent: The spec must include a 'For DISC-016/DISC-017 implementer' callout section listing: (1) exact prop shape `CuratedPill = { label: string; routeId: string }`, (2) required token substitutions as a find/replace table, (3) testID pattern `discovery-suggestion-pill-{routeId}` (already in code at line 105, confirm), (4) `onSelectRoute` prop already wired at line 103.

end_state.must_not_observe: File not found OR any section missing OR any raw hex color not cross-referenced to a token path

design_tasks.title: Curated-route chat-card variant spec (composite score as %/bars on 0–1; distinct from planned-trip routing card)
design_tasks.outcome: A written layout spec and `variant='curated'` prop plan exists at `.spec/design/sprint-01/curated-route-chat-card-spec.md`, naming exact token paths and the score-rendering formula, verifiable by `pnpm tokens:validate` and on-device inspection at the sprint gate.
critical_constraints: NEVER specify score as a 0–100 integer or a 0–1 decimal in display — the ONLY valid display form is `Math.round(score * 100) + '%'` (e.g. score=0.73 → '73%'). The spec must name this formula explicitly and flag any display of raw 0–1 or 0–100 integers as a bug.

NEVER show start→end leg labels on the curated card — the card shows route name, mileage, and archetype badge; the `{startLabel} → {endLabel}` row from `RouteAttachmentCard` is a planned-trip pattern and must NOT appear on the curated variant.
NEVER specify a hardcoded hex for any color property — every color must be a token path from `tokens/semantic/semantic.tokens.json` or `tokens/semantic/colors.tokens.json`.
Spec is a WRITE to `.spec/design/sprint-01/curated-route-chat-card-spec.md` ONLY — do NOT modify `route-attachment-card.tsx` or any app source.
Scores arrive on the 0–1 scale from `useCuratedDiscovery` and must be carried through unmodified to the rendering formula — the spec must note this and prohibit rescaling in the hook or store.

specification.deliverable: `.spec/design/sprint-01/curated-route-chat-card-spec.md` — a structured spec with five sections: (1) Score rendering contract; (2) Layout spec for the `curated` variant; (3) Visual differentiation table vs the existing `full` variant of `RouteAttachmentCard`; (4) `variant='curated'` prop plan (new prop or new component decision); (5) Token path table.

score_rendering_contract.formula: Math.round(score * 100) + '%' where score is the 0–1 float from `useCuratedDiscovery` / the agent curated-discovery tool
score_rendering_contract.composite_display: Large percentage badge using `semantic.type.title.lg` (fontSize 17, fontWeight 600) in `semantic.color.primary.default` (#EE7C2B copper)
score_rendering_contract.bar_rendering: Each dimension score rendered as a `ScoreDimensionBar` (as specified in 10-design-system.md §2): track `semantic.color.surface.inset`, fill `semantic.color.primary.default`, height `semantic.space.xs × 2 = 8dp`, value `Math.round(score*100)+'%'` via `semantic.type.label.sm` + JetBrains Mono (instrument font)
forbidden_displays: 0% when score is non-zero (score-field mapping bug — see DATA-008b)

curated_card_layout_spec.row_1_route_name: Route name as `semantic.type.title.lg` (17/600), color `semantic.color.onSurface.default` — NOT the `{startLabel} → {endLabel}` format
curated_card_layout_spec.row_2_mileage_archetype: Mileage in miles (`${Math.round(distanceMi)}mi`) + archetype badge (existing `Badge` component, variant='secondary') in a `flexDirection: row, gap: semantic.space.sm` layout
curated_card_layout_spec.row_3_composite_score: Composite score as `Math.round(compositeScore*100)+'%'` displayed prominently; optionally followed by 2–3 dimension score bars (curvature, scenic, technical) using `ScoreDimensionBar`
curated_card_layout_spec.touch_target: full card is tappable; height must reach `semantic.control.minTouchTarget` (44pt) minimum via natural content or explicit minHeight

visual_differentiation_table.description: The spec must include a side-by-side table: column 1 = 'Planned-trip `full` variant (RouteAttachmentCard)', column 2 = 'Curated `curated` variant'. Key differentiators: start→end labels (present/absent), score bars (absent/present), archetype badge (absent/present), route name prominence (secondary/primary).

prop_plan.recommendation: Add `variant='curated'` to `RouteAttachmentCardProps` alongside the existing `'compact' | 'full'`. This avoids a new component file and keeps the tap→map loop machinery intact. Alternatively, a new `CuratedRouteCard` component is acceptable if the implementer (DISC-020) determines the conditional branching inside `RouteAttachmentCard` would become too complex. The spec must document both options and state the preference.
new_props_if_extending: curatedRoute?: { name: string; distanceMi: number; archetype: RouteArchetype; compositeScore: number; curvatureScore?: number; scenicScore?: number; technicalScore?: number }

design_tasks.title: No-route empty/invite home-state spec (testID home-empty-state): discovery-invite copy + empty-catalog messaging over surface.glass scrim @ 72%
design_tasks.outcome: A written spec exists at `.spec/design/sprint-01/home-empty-state-spec.md` defining the no-route empty/invite overlay (testID `home-empty-state`), with exact token paths, copy strings, zIndex rule, and gating condition, verifiable by `pnpm tokens:validate` and on-device inspection at the sprint gate.
critical_constraints: NEVER specify `zIndex` higher than the `ChatInput` component (which uses `zIndex: 20` in StyleSheet) — the empty-state overlay MUST render below the ChatInput so suggestion pill taps pass through; spec must explicitly state `zIndex: 10` or lower and explain why.
NEVER use hardcoded hex for any color — every color must reference a token path; discovery-invite copy color is `semantic.color.onSurface.muted` with italic style.

NEVER show the empty-state overlay when (a) a route IS on the map or (b) transcript messages exist — the gating condition must be a two-clause AND: `!hasActiveRoute AND transcriptMessages.length === 0`.
Spec is a WRITE to `.spec/design/sprint-01/home-empty-state-spec.md` ONLY — do NOT modify `index.tsx` or any app source.
The overlay uses `surface.glass` (72% alpha token) as its background — NOT the `surface.overlay` (92% alpha) token.

specification.deliverable: `.spec/design/sprint-01/home-empty-state-spec.md` — a structured spec with four sections: (1) Gating condition; (2) Copy strings and typography; (3) Layout and visual spec; (4) zIndex and interaction model.

gating_condition.hasActiveRoute_source: derived from `!!agentActiveOption` in `index.tsx` line 257 — already available as a prop to ChatInput
gating_condition.transcriptMessages_source: rawTranscriptMessages filtered array from `useQuery(api.db.sessionMessages.list, ...)` in index.tsx
gating_condition.testID: home-empty-state (the overlay View or its wrapper)

copy_strings.empty_catalog_condition: shown only when `useCuratedDiscovery` returns `isEmpty === true` (routes === []); hidden during loading (`isLoading === true`) and hidden when routes exist

layout_and_visual.container_background: semantic.color.surface.glass (light: rgba(253,251,248,0.72), dark: rgba(45,34,24,0.72)) — NOT surface.overlay (92% alpha)
layout_and_visual.positioning: absolute, bottom positioned above ChatInput; `bottom` value = ChatInput height (approximately 90pt including suggestion chips) + semantic.space.md (12pt) margin; OR `marginBottom` from ChatInput using the component's own bottomOffset prop
layout_and_visual.alignment: centered horizontally via `alignSelf: 'center'` or `left: 0, right: 0` with `alignItems: 'center'`

zindex_and_interaction.rationale: ChatInput is at zIndex: 20 in chat-input.tsx StyleSheet line ~418; the empty-state must be below ChatInput so it does not intercept pill tap events; suggestion chips (testID `chat-input-suggestion-chips`) must remain tappable
zindex_and_interaction.pointer_events: The overlay View should have `pointerEvents='none'` since it is purely informational and must not block taps on the suggestion chips area

design_tasks.title: Footer full-chat button distinction spec: icon/shape vs send, ≥44pt, active-state token, compliance audit of chat-input-chat-view-button
design_tasks.outcome: A written compliance audit and spec exists at `.spec/design/sprint-01/footer-chat-button-spec.md` for the `chat-input-chat-view-button`, confirming or correcting its icon shape, size, active-state token, and border/elevation against the spec — verifiable by `pnpm tokens:validate` and on-device sprint gate.
critical_constraints: NEVER specify that the full-chat button and the send button share the same icon — they must be visually distinct affordances; the spec must name different icons for each and explain why the distinction matters for one-handed UX.
NEVER specify a touch target below 44pt — `semantic.control.minTouchTarget` (44) is the floor; the current `chatViewBtnSize = 48` already passes but must be cited via the token.

NEVER specify the active-state background as a hardcoded hex — active state MUST use `semantic.color.primary.default` (#EE7C2B copper); the current code at line 385 uses this token correctly; the spec must confirm it.
Spec is a WRITE to `.spec/design/sprint-01/footer-chat-button-spec.md` ONLY — do NOT modify `chat-input.tsx` or any app source.
The compliance audit must cite specific line numbers from `chat-input.tsx` for each property it evaluates.

specification.deliverable: `.spec/design/sprint-01/footer-chat-button-spec.md` — a structured compliance audit + spec with three sections: (1) Required spec for the full-chat button; (2) Compliance audit of current implementation (chat-input.tsx lines 373-403); (3) Gap summary (PASS/FAIL per property).

required_spec.testID: chat-input-chat-view-button (already in code at line 392 — confirm)
required_spec.purpose: Navigation affordance: opens the full chat transcript view; categorically different from the send action
required_spec.icon_when_inactive: 'chat-outline' (react-native-paper Material Design icon) — navigation/mode icon, NOT 'arrow-right' (which is the send icon)
required_spec.icon_when_active: 'map-outline' — signals 'return to map', i.e. closing the chat transcript
required_spec.button_size: 48 × 48pt (chatViewBtnSize = 48 in current code) — must be cited against semantic.control.minTouchTarget (44); 48 ≥ 44 → PASS
required_spec.border_radius: chatViewBtnSize / 2 = 24pt → circular; equivalent to semantic.radius.full (9999) at this size
required_spec.background_inactive: semantic.color.surface.default — translucent glass surface (the button sits over the map); current code uses `semantic.color.surface.default` at line 384
required_spec.background_active: semantic.color.primary.default (#EE7C2B copper) — current code line 383 uses this token; PASS
required_spec.border_color_inactive: semantic.color.border.default — current code line 387 uses this token; PASS
required_spec.border_color_active: semantic.color.primary.default — current code line 386 uses this token; PASS
required_spec.icon_color_inactive: semantic.color.onSurface.muted — current code line 401 uses this token; PASS
required_spec.icon_color_active: semantic.color.onPrimary.default — current code line 400 uses this token; PASS
required_spec.elevation: semantic.elevation[2] — current code line 388-390 applies `...semantic.elevation[2]`; PASS

compliance_audit_instructions.evaluate_each_property: For each property in the Required Spec, locate the implementing line in `chat-input.tsx` (lines 373-403 for the chat-view button), compare to spec, and emit PASS or FAIL with the line number.
compliance_audit_instructions.icon_distinction_audit: The send button uses `Icon source='arrow-right'` (line 362); the chat-view button uses `Icon source={chatMode ? 'map-outline' : 'chat-outline'}` (line 397-399). These are categorically different — PASS. Spec must confirm this distinction is sufficient for one-handed UX and document why: arrow-right implies 'submit/forward', map/chat-outline implies 'navigate to mode'.
compliance_audit_instructions.active_state_audit: Line 383: `chatMode ? semantic.color.primary.default : semantic.color.surface.default` → active background is copper → PASS.

expected_gaps: The spec should identify: the `chatViewBtnSize` variable uses a magic number `48` rather than a token; recommend `Math.max(semantic.control.minTouchTarget, 48)` or a dedicated `semantic.size.large` (48pt) token reference for future Copper Navigator alignment
The icon size uses magic number `iconSize + 2 = 20` (line 243, 398) rather than `semantic.iconSize.medium` (20) — flag as a gap even though the value is correct, because the token path should be used

acceptance_criteria.given: The compliance audit size/touch target row
acceptance_criteria.when: The audit verdict is read

acceptance_criteria.name: On-device: full-chat button visually distinct from send at sprint gate
acceptance_criteria.given: DISC-018 is merged; real device

acceptance_criteria.then: The `chat-input-chat-view-button` icon is clearly different from the `chat-input-send-button` icon; tapping it opens the full chat view; active state shows copper background

design_enrichments.sprint_task_title: FIX: tapping a suggestion card plots the already-fetched curated route on the map directly
notes: Read `.spec/design/sprint-01/suggestion-card-spec.md` before implementing the tap handler — the chip variant style props are specified there with exact token paths.
The `CuratedPill` type (`{ label: string; routeId: string }`) and `onSelectRoute` prop are already defined in `chat-input.tsx` lines 36-39 and 102-103; do not redefine them.
The existing `TouchableOpacity` at chat-input.tsx line 100 already has `testID={routeId ? \`discovery-suggestion-pill-${routeId}\` : undefined}` — preserve this testID pattern.
Chip background MUST become `semantic.color.surface.glass` (not `semantic.color.surface.default`) per DESIGN-S01-001; this is the primary token gap the spec identifies.
Chip border MUST become `semantic.color.border.glass` (not `semantic.color.border.default`) per DESIGN-S01-001.
The `onSelectRoute` handler at line 103 already invokes the prop — the fix is in the caller (`index.tsx` `handleSelectCuratedRoute`) to plot directly rather than send a chat message.

design_enrichments.sprint_task_title: FIX: suggestion slot shows curated cards (or nothing while loading/empty) — never the generic IDLE_SUGGESTIONS planning prompts
notes: Read `.spec/design/sprint-01/suggestion-card-spec.md` for the chip variant spec before implementing the curated-vs-generic gate.
Read `.spec/design/sprint-01/home-empty-state-spec.md` for the empty-state overlay that accompanies this task (copy strings, zIndex, surface.glass token).
The gating condition in `chat-input.tsx` line 268 already has `!hasActiveRoute` — extend it to also replace the `IDLE_SUGGESTIONS` fallback with curated pills or nothing (not the generic strings).
Empty-state overlay (testID `home-empty-state`) must render at `zIndex: 10` — below `ChatInput`'s `zIndex: 20` — so it does not intercept pill taps; use `pointerEvents='none'` on the overlay View.

Discovery-invite copy: 'Discover roads near you' (`semantic.type.body.md`, `fontStyle: italic`, `semantic.color.onSurface.muted`). Empty-catalog copy: 'No routes near you yet' (`semantic.type.body.sm`, `semantic.color.onSurface.muted`) — shown only when `isEmpty === true` from `useCuratedDiscovery`.
Surface.glass token for the overlay background: `color.surface.glass` (light: rgba(253,251,248,0.72), dark: rgba(45,34,24,0.72)) — NOT `color.surface.overlay` (92% alpha).

design_enrichments.sprint_task_title: Render chat-driven curated routes as transcript cards (curated variant; score as %/bars) + verify the card→map→return-to-map loop with centroid fallback
notes: Read `.spec/design/sprint-01/curated-route-chat-card-spec.md` before implementing `variant='curated'` — it contains the score rendering formula, layout rows, and visual differentiation table.
Score rendering formula: `Math.round(score * 100) + '%'` where `score` is the 0–1 float from the agent curated-discovery tool. NEVER display raw 0–1 decimal or raw 0–100 integer.
The curated card must NOT show a `{startLabel} → {endLabel}` row — that is the planned-trip pattern; curated shows route name + mileage + archetype badge.

Use `ScoreDimensionBar` for score bars (spec in `.spec/prds/mvp/09-technical-requirements/10-design-system.md §2`): track `semantic.color.surface.inset`, fill `semantic.color.primary.default`, height 8dp.
Composite score display: `semantic.type.title.lg` (fontSize 17, fontWeight 600) + `semantic.color.primary.default` (#EE7C2B copper).
The spec recommends `variant='curated'` on the existing `RouteAttachmentCard` rather than a new component, to preserve the tap→map loop machinery at `route-attachment-card.tsx` lines 119-126.
testID remains `route-attachment-card` per `07-ui-infrastructure.md §6`.

DATA-008b must land before or in parallel: if `compositeScore` is 0 from the flat-field mapping bug, the card will display '0%' — this is a data bug, not a render bug; the render formula is correct when the data is correct.

design_enrichments.sprint_task_title: Verify/harden the footer 'open full chat' button (distinct from send) + suggestion-card visibility keyed to 'no active route on the map'
notes: Read `.spec/design/sprint-01/footer-chat-button-spec.md` — it is a compliance audit of the current implementation; fix only the FAIL rows.

Expected FAILs (minor): chatViewBtnSize uses magic number 48 (should reference `semantic.control.minTouchTarget` or `semantic.size.large`); iconSize+2=20 should reference `semantic.iconSize.medium` (20).

Expected PASSes (do not change): active background `semantic.color.primary.default` (line 383), active border `semantic.color.primary.default` (line 386), inactive border `semantic.color.border.default` (line 387), elevation `semantic.elevation[2]` (line 388), active icon `semantic.color.onPrimary.default` (line 400), inactive icon `semantic.color.onSurface.muted` (line 401).
Icon distinction: send='arrow-right' vs chat-view='chat-outline'/'map-outline' — this is already PASS; do NOT change the icons unless the audit finds a regression.
testID `chat-input-chat-view-button` at line 392 — do not remove or rename.

design_system_status.overall_readiness: The token system is READY for implementation — `tokens/semantic/colors.tokens.json` and `tokens/semantic/semantic.tokens.json` define all required paths. Two token-usage gaps exist in the current source code; they do not require new token definitions, only correct usage of existing tokens.

gaps.description: surface.glass token exists in `colors.tokens.json` (color.surface.glass) but the `SuggestionChips` component in `chat-input.tsx` uses `semantic.color.surface.default` (the opaque surface) for chip background instead of the 72%-alpha glass token. This makes curated chips opaque rather than glassmorphic, violating 10-design-system.md §1.

border.glass token exists in `colors.tokens.json` but curated chip border uses `semantic.color.border.default` (paper-400 / 12%-white-alpha). On a glass-background chip over the map, the default border is too opaque; the glass border (`rgba(255,255,255,0.55)` / `rgba(242,238,232,0.22)`) is the correct token for a glassmorphic affordance.

The `semantic.color.accent` token path is accessed in chat-input.tsx line 129 as `semantic.color.accent?.default ?? '#EE7C2B'` with an optional-chain guard and a hardcoded hex fallback. The token IS defined in `semantic.tokens.json` for both light and dark modes. The optional-chain and hex fallback are unnecessary and should be replaced with a direct `semantic.color.accent.default` access.
gaps.required_value: semantic.color.accent.default (token is stable — defined in semantic.tokens.json lines 137-150 for both themes)

gaps.description: chatViewBtnSize (48) and iconSize (18) in chat-input.tsx lines 243-244 are magic numbers. semantic.control.minTouchTarget (44) and semantic.iconSize.medium (20) exist as tokens. The values are close but not token-sourced. This is a readability/maintainability gap, not a visual defect; values produce correct behavior.
gaps.required_value: Derive from tokens or document the intentional deviation (48 > 44 for comfortable one-handed tap; 20 = iconSize.medium which is iconSize+2=20)

gaps.description: No `onSurface.muted` sub-key exists on `semantic.color.onSurface` in `semantic.tokens.json` — the file only defines `onSurface.default`. The code references `semantic.color.onSurface.muted` in multiple places (e.g. chat-input.tsx line 304, route-attachment-card.tsx line 211). This path resolves at runtime via `useSemanticTheme()` which may map it from a different token source. The design task specs reference this path; implementers must verify via `pnpm type-check` that the path resolves without TypeScript errors before assuming it is safe.
gaps.required_value: Either: confirm `useSemanticTheme()` maps `onSurface.muted` from `color.content.tertiary` or similar; OR add `onSurface.muted` to `semantic.tokens.json` explicitly. The spec tasks reference this path as an existing working token — verify at the sprint gate.
gaps.fix_owned_by: Verify only — if type-check fails, `convex-implementer` or `react-native-ui-implementer` must add the token; if it passes, no action needed.

ready_tokens: semantic.color.accent.default (#EE7C2B copper-500) — defined in both light/dark in semantic.tokens.json
semantic.color.surface.glass — defined in colors.tokens.json (light: rgba(253,251,248,0.72), dark: rgba(45,34,24,0.72))
semantic.color.primary.default (#EE7C2B) — active state, score bars, composite score text
semantic.type.body.md, semantic.type.body.sm, semantic.type.title.lg, semantic.type.label.sm — all defined
semantic.space.xs (4), semantic.space.sm (8), semantic.space.md (12), semantic.space.lg (16), semantic.space.xl (24) — defined
semantic.elevation[1] through semantic.elevation[5], semantic.elevation[8] — defined for both themes
setup_needed_actions: Verify `useSemanticTheme()` maps `semantic.color.onSurface.muted` at runtime — run `pnpm type-check` after DISC-016 implementation to confirm no TypeScript errors on this path before marking DESIGN-S01-001 complete
